# terrible_cisco_aci_aci_filter_entry (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `arp_flag` (String) The arp flag to use when the ether_type is arp. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description for the Filter Entry.
- `destination_port` (String) Used to set both destination start and end ports to the same value when ip_protocol is tcp or udp. Accepted values are any valid TCP/UDP port range. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `destination_port_end` (String) Used to set the destination end port when ip_protocol is tcp or udp. Accepted values are any valid TCP/UDP port range. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `destination_port_start` (String) Used to set the destination start port when ip_protocol is tcp or udp. Accepted values are any valid TCP/UDP port range. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `entry` (String) Then name of the Filter Entry.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `ether_type` (String) The Ethernet type. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `filter` (String) The name of Filter that the entry should belong to.
- `icmp6_msg_type` (String) ICMPv6 message type; used when ip_protocol is icmpv6. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `icmp_msg_type` (String) ICMPv4 message type; used when ip_protocol is icmp. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ip_protocol` (String) The IP Protocol type when ether_type is ip. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `match_only_fragments` (Boolean) The match only packet fragments of the filter entry. When enabled `true` the rule applies to any fragments with offset greater than 0 (all fragments except first). When disabled `false` it applies to all packets (including all fragments) The APIC defaults to `false` when unset during creation.
- `name_alias` (String) The alias for the current object. This relates to the nameAlias field in ACI.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `source_port` (String) Used to set both source start and end ports to the same value when ip_protocol is tcp or udp. Accepted values are any valid TCP/UDP port range. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `source_port_end` (String) Used to set the source end port when ip_protocol is tcp or udp. Accepted values are any valid TCP/UDP port range. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `source_port_start` (String) Used to set the source start port when ip_protocol is tcp or udp. Accepted values are any valid TCP/UDP port range. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `state` (String) present, absent, query
- `stateful` (Boolean) Determines the statefulness of the filter entry.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tcp_flags` (String) The TCP flags of the filter entry. The TCP `established` cannot be combined with other tcp rules. The APIC defaults to `unspecified` when unset during creation.
- `tenant` (String) The name of the tenant.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `current` (String) The existing configuration from the APIC after the module has finished
- `error` (String) The error information as returned from the APIC
- `filter_string` (String) The filter string used for the request
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `method` (String) The HTTP method used for the request to the APIC
- `previous` (String) The original configuration from the APIC before the module has started
- `proposed` (String) The assembled configuration from the user-provided parameters
- `raw` (String) The raw output returned by the APIC REST API (xml or json)
- `response` (String) The HTTP response from the APIC
- `sent` (String) The actual/minimal configuration pushed to the APIC
- `status` (Number) The HTTP status from the APIC
- `url` (String) The HTTP url used for the request to the APIC
